Turn the probe or drag c1's round handle: watch every score rise, fall, or flip sign, live.

{#each rows as r (r.id)} {/each} {#if tied}tied{/if} {#if cheatVictim} ⚑ {cheatVictim.name} matches the probe better, but {c1row.name} outscores it on size {:else if shortChanged} ⚑ {c1row.name} matches best, but {leaderName} wins on size {/if}

Click any bar or arrow to inspect it. The dashed lines are that arrow's two entries (its loudness and its speed), the numbers the recipe multiplies.

the whole recipe, live for {sel.name}: loud {f(probe.x)}·{f(sel.x)} + fast {f(probe.y)}·{f(sel.y)} = {f(sel.px)} + {f(sel.py)} = {f1(sel.score)} multiply the matched entries, add the products. The lilac slice of each bar is its loud product, the mint slice its fast product; stacked, they are the score.

content.text: Good, no stray `glow` variable; the error line reference was stale context. The file now has the sole build defect fixed. Let me verify the rest of the file doesn't contain other `{@const}` misplacements or raw hex colors.

file.content:
<text x={CX - (d.cls === 'p' ? 4 : 32)} y={cl(tip.py) - 3} class="coord mono {d.cls}" text-anchor="end" aria-hidden="true">{f(d.y)}</text>
				{/each}

				<!-- candidates -->
				{#each rows as r (r.id)}
					{@const a = arrow(r.x, r.y)}
					{@const lp = labelPos(r.angle, r.len, 26)}
					<g
						class="cand"
						class:sel={selected === r.id}
						class:hov={hovered === r.id}
						onpointerdown={() => (selected = r.id)}
						role="button"
						tabindex="-1"
						aria-label={`select ${r.name}`}
					>
						<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft cand-stroke" />
						<polygon points={a.head} class="cand-fill" />
						<text
							x={Math.min(Math.max(lp.px, 44), S - 48)}
							y={Math.min(Math.max(lp.py, 16), S - 10)}
							class="lab song-lab halo"
							class:sel-lab={selected === r.id}
							aria-hidden="true">{r.name}</text>
					</g>
				{/each}

				<!-- c1 length handle -->
				<circle
					cx={c1HandlePos.px}

content.text:
Fixed. The rest of the file uses only `var(--...)` tokens, no hex literals, and no other misplaced `{@const}`.

DONE Fixed the Svelte build error in DotAlignment.svelte by moving the misplaced `{@const lp = labelPos(...)}` out of the `<g>` element and up to be a direct child of the `{#each rows}` block (alongside the existing `{@const a = arrow(...)}`), resolving the `{@const}` placement rule violation that broke the build.

result:
Fixed. The rest of the file uses only `var(--...)` tokens, no hex literals, and no other misplaced `{@const}`.

DONE Fixed the Svelte build error in DotAlignment.svelte by moving the misplaced `{@const lp = labelPos(...)}` out of the `<g>` element and up to be a direct child of the `{#each rows}` block (alongside the existing `{@const a = arrow(...)}`), resolving the `{@const}` placement rule violation that broke the build.
